supporting_evidence: 81-year-old female with fall at home — most common presentation in this age group
Osteoporosis and age are primary risk factors for falls
Vital signs stable with no acute derangement
Mechanism described (fell and hit head on floor) consistent with simple mechanical fall

candidates.diagnosis: Hip contusion or occult hip fracture
supporting_evidence: Patient reports hip pain after fall
Osteoporosis significantly increases fracture risk from low-energy mechanisms
Occult hip fractures may not be evident on initial X-ray — MRI may be required
81-year-old female with osteoporosis is the textbook demographic for hip fracture
Head strike on floor in patient on warfarin anticoagulation
Warfarin-associated intracranial hemorrhage has 40-60% mortality
Patient reports dizziness which may be early neurological sign
Age-related cerebral atrophy increases subdural hematoma risk
Dizziness reported — may indicate pre-syncopal episode rather than post-traumatic symptom
Atrial fibrillation can cause intermittent cerebral hypoperfusion
Enalapril may contribute to orthostatic hypotension in elderly
Must distinguish between fall causing dizziness vs dizziness causing fall
Head strike mechanism implies cervical spine loading
Osteoporosis increases risk of vertebral fracture from minor trauma
Elderly patients may have pre-existing cervical spondylosis masking acute injury
Canadian C-spine Rule: age >65 is a high-risk criterion

reasoning: 81-year-old female with osteoporosis and atrial fibrillation on warfarin presenting after a fall with head strike and hip pain. Simple mechanical fall is most likely given the mechanism and age group. Hip contusion or occult fracture is significant given osteoporosis. Intracranial hemorrhage is flagged as a don't-miss given warfarin use — the combination of anticoagulation and head trauma demands CT imaging regardless of clinical presentation. Syncope must be considered as the cause rather than consequence of the fall. C-spine injury is a don't-miss given the mechanism and osteoporosis.
